notes:
STRICTLY ADDITIVE seamless edge-walk traversal primitive for the legacy 2D game. Sibling to sceneTransition.js (transitionTo). Existing teleport flow remains the only path used by world-map travel, doorways, and named-spawn jumps; consumer scenes choose which primitive to call based on intent.

=== HARD-CONSTRAINT VERIFICATION ===

1. sceneTransition.js NOT modified:
   $ git diff --stat HEAD -- src/renderer/game/systems/sceneTransition.js
   <empty output> — file is byte-identical to HEAD.

2. No new scene.start call sites outside the new module:
   $ grep -rn 'scene\.start\|this\.scene\.start' src/renderer/game/ | grep -v seamlessTraversal.js | wc -l
   8
   Matches the orchestrator-captured pre-dispatch baseline of 8. Sites:
     - GarageScene.js:144  (existing fade→start to NeighborhoodScene)
     - NeighborhoodScene.js:638  (existing fade→start to GarageScene)
     - StreetBlockScene.js:355  (existing WorldMapScene jump)
     - WorldMapScene.js:1327  (existing entryScene jump)
     - WorldMapScene.js:1349  (existing origin scene return)
     - ZuzuGarageScene.js:201  (existing MaterialLabScene jump)
     - ZuzuGarageScene.js:249  (existing ThermalRigScene jump)
     - sceneTransition.js:68  (the canonical teleport flow)
   None of these 8 was touched.

3. node --check passed on the new module (SYNTAX_OK).

4. Module loads cleanly with all 4 required exports:
   Missing: 0 []
   SCENE_ADJACENCY.NeighborhoodScene: {"east":{"to":"DryWashScene","entrySide":"west"}}
   typeof performSeamlessTransition: function

5. npm run build: PASSED (built in 11.28s, 878 modules transformed, no errors).

=== FINAL API SURFACE ===

File: src/renderer/game/systems/seamlessTraversal.js

  export const SCENE_ADJACENCY: AdjacencyMap
    Plain JS object map. Mutable / extendable — consumer agents append
    entries by mutating it (SCENE_ADJACENCY.X = { ...SCENE_ADJACENCY.X, east: {...} }).
    Pattern documented in JSDoc on the export.

  export function performSeamlessTransition(scene, edge, player): void
    Looks up SCENE_ADJACENCY[scene.scene.key]?.[edge]; if absent, console.warn
    + no-op (safe fail). Otherwise captures player velocity (sprite.body.velocity)
    + facing, then calls scene.scene.start(targetSceneKey, { seamless: true,
    entryEdge, vx, vy, facing }). Local _seamlessTransitioning guard prevents
    re-entry without colliding with the teleport flow's _transitioning flag.
    Does NOT do spawn-point lookup, fade transition, or save trigger.

  export function applySeamlessEntry(scene, data): boolean
    No-op (returns false) when data?.entryEdge is absent, so scenes with
    normal init paths pass through unchanged. When present: computes spawn
    (x,y) from entryEdge + scene.getWorldSize?.() / physics.world.bounds /
    scene.scale fallback chain, repositions the player (Player.setPosition →
    body.reset → sprite.setPosition fallback chain), restores vx/vy onto the
    physics body, and restores player.facing if provided. Returns true on apply.

  export function attachEdgeSensor(scene, player, callback): { zones, destroy }
    Wires 4 thin (8px) physics-overlap zones, one per cardinal edge of the
    world bounds, each firing callback(edge) where edge ∈ 'north'|'south'|
    'east'|'west'. OPT-IN: scenes that don't call this helper see zero
    behavior change. Returns { zones, destroy } for optional teardown.
    Callback exceptions are caught and console.warn'd so a misbehaving
    consumer never crashes the scene.

=== ADJACENCY MAP SHAPE + SEEDED ENTRIES ===

Shape:  AdjacencyMap = Object<sceneKey, Object<edge, { to, entrySide, options? }>>
        edge ∈ 'north' | 'south' | 'east' | 'west'.

Seeded entries (verbatim from the source):

  export const SCENE_ADJACENCY = {
    NeighborhoodScene: {
      east: { to: 'DryWashScene', entrySide: 'west' },
      // … other edges added by consumer agents as scenes adopt the API.
    },
  };

This matches the spec's First-cycle-goal item 1 exactly. The seed lets
phaser-dry-wash-scene consume the adjacency without round-tripping through
this agent — DryWashScene only needs to register its own west→Neighborhood
back-edge: SCENE_ADJACENCY.DryWashScene = { west: { to: 'NeighborhoodScene', entrySide: 'east' } }.

=== DESIGN NOTES ===

- Used a separate _seamlessTransitioning flag (NOT scene._transitioning) to
  avoid colliding with the teleport flow's guard. Both flows can co-exist
  on the same scene instance without stepping on each other.
- Player kinematics capture is defensive: handles missing .sprite, missing
  .body, missing .velocity. Returns zero velocities + null facing rather
  than throwing.
- World-size resolution priority: scene.getWorldSize?.() → physics.world.bounds
  → scene.scale.{width,height} → 800x600 default. Matches LocalSceneBase
  conventions so adopting scenes Just Work.
- Edge sensors are static physics bodies (overlap, not collide) inset by
  half-thickness so the body lies inside the world rect.
- Entry inset (32px) keeps the spawned player from immediately re-triggering
  the same edge sensor on arrival.
- Every export has full JSDoc (params, returns, behavior) so consumer agents
  can read the contract from the .js file alone — no separate spec doc
  needed.
- next_agent_suggestions: phaser-dry-wash-scene + phaser-bridge-construction
  are parallel-safe per the pod yaml — neither modifies this file or each
  other's primary surface.
